description:
You are acting as an automation-workflow specialist who has to set up a very small demo for teaching newcomers how to manage “dotenv” files and session-wide environment variables in Bash.

Overall goal
------------
Create a reproducible workspace at

    /home/user/workflows/env-demo

that contains:

1. A dotenv file named “.env” holding three key–value pairs.
2. An executable Bash helper script “run_workflow.sh” that
      • loads the variables from the “.env” file,
      • **does not** hard-code the values inside the script,
      • writes them into a log file called “run.log” in **exactly** the format described below.
3. A shell snippet “session_exports.sh” that permanently exports the same variables for interactive sessions.

When all steps are finished, the **file system state** must look exactly like this:

    /home/user/workflows/env-demo/
    ├── .env
    ├── run_workflow.sh      (must be executable)
    ├── run.log              (will be created by the script itself)
    └── session_exports.sh

Precise, testable requirements
------------------------------
A. “/home/user/workflows/env-demo/.env”

   • Must consist of exactly three non-empty lines, each a simple KEY=value pair, no quotes or spaces.  
   • The lines **must appear in this order**:

        APP_NAME=EnvAutomation
        APP_VERSION=1.2.3
        API_KEY=abcd1234secret

   • The file must end with a trailing newline (“\n”).

B. “/home/user/workflows/env-demo/run_workflow.sh”

   • Must start with the she-bang line “#!/usr/bin/env bash”.  
   • Must make itself independent of the caller’s current directory (i.e. internally switch to its own directory).  
   • Must read the variables by “sourcing” the “.env” file (you may use “set -a”/“set +a” or an equivalent safe pattern).  
   • After sourcing, it must create (or overwrite) the file “run.log” in the same directory with exactly four lines and a final newline:

        2023-05-04T12:00:00Z
        APP_NAME=<value_of_APP_NAME>
        APP_VERSION=<value_of_APP_VERSION>
        API_KEY=<value_of_API_KEY>

     where the three “<value_of_…>” placeholders are the values that were actually read from “.env”.  
   • The timestamp line (“2023-05-04T12:00:00Z”) is literal and MUST be written exactly as shown, so automated tests have a deterministic value to compare against.  
   • The file itself must be given the executable bit (e.g., mode 755 or 750; any mode where the owner can execute is accepted).

C. “/home/user/workflows/env-demo/session_exports.sh”

   • Must contain **exactly** these three lines (plus a trailing newline):

        export APP_NAME=EnvAutomation
        export APP_VERSION=1.2.3
        export API_KEY=abcd1234secret

D. “/home/user/workflows/env-demo/run.log”

   • Must be present at the end of the task (create it by actually running “run_workflow.sh” once).  
   • Contents and ordering must match the specification in section B verbatim.  
   • Must end with a single trailing newline character.

Hints & constraints for the agent
---------------------------------
1. You do NOT have root access; stay inside /home/user.  
2. Use only POSIX-compatible shell constructs; no external dot-env helper libraries are available.  
3. After everything is ready, **remember to execute the script once** so that “run.log” is created, because the automated checker will look for it.  
4. Make sure the directory structure is created before trying to place the files there, and set file permissions as required.  
5. Do not include extra console output in the log file—write ONLY the four requested lines.

If the resulting files, permissions, and contents match the rules above, the task is considered complete.
truth:
Expected final artefacts and their exact contents:

1. Path: /home/user/workflows/env-demo/.env  
   Mode: at least 644 (rw-r--r--)  
   Content (with final newline):
APP_NAME=EnvAutomation
APP_VERSION=1.2.3
API_KEY=abcd1234secret

2. Path: /home/user/workflows/env-demo/run_workflow.sh  
   Mode: must include owner-execute (e.g., 755)  
   Content should:  
     - start with "#!/usr/bin/env bash"  
     - change to its own directory (e.g., cd "$(dirname "$0")")  
     - call source .env (with or without set -a/+a)  
     - write exactly the four required lines (see run.log below) to run.log (use >, not >>).  
   There is no exact byte-for-byte enforcement on comments/whitespace, only on functional behaviour, she-bang, and that the file is executable.

3. Path: /home/user/workflows/env-demo/session_exports.sh  
   Mode: at least 644  
   Content (with final newline):
export APP_NAME=EnvAutomation
export APP_VERSION=1.2.3
export API_KEY=abcd1234secret

4. Path: /home/user/workflows/env-demo/run.log  
   Mode: at least 644  
   Content (with final newline):
2023-05-04T12:00:00Z
APP_NAME=EnvAutomation
APP_VERSION=1.2.3
API_KEY=abcd1234secret

Directory: /home/user/workflows/env-demo must exist and be writable by “user”.
